description: Web UI Phase 2 (admin): write actions on the embedded console at /console/. Bucket admin (Create/Delete/Versioning/Object-Lock/Lifecycle/CORS/Policy/ACL/Inventory/Logging), IAM admin (Users/AccessKeys/ManagedPolicies/Attach), object actions (Upload via per-part presign URLs/Delete/Tags/Retention/LegalHold), Multipart watchdog, Audit-log viewer, Settings page (with JWT secret rotation), BackendPresign toggle (s3-over-s3 only), S3-backend config exposure on Settings, Playwright admin.spec.ts. All endpoints sit under /admin/v1/* with the existing JWT session-cookie + SigV4 auth chain. Monaco editor lazy-loaded for JSON editors so non-editor pages stay <=500 KiB gzipped. Browser uploads via per-part presigned URLs minted server-side (operator AK/SK never reaches browser). 22 stories. Cycle ends with merge ralph/web-ui-admin -> main per memory/feedback_ralph_branch_policy.md. See tasks/prd-web-ui-admin.md for full AC.

userStories.title: CreateBucket dialog
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a modal that creates a new bucket with a name validator, region selector, versioning toggle, and Object-Lock default toggle so I do not have to drop to aws-cli for the common case.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /admin/v1/buckets accepts {name, region, versioning, object_lock_enabled} and calls meta.Store.CreateBucket. Returns 201 + bucket details on success, 409 BucketAlreadyExists on collision, 400 InvalidBucketName on reserved name (uses internal/s3api.validBucketName already on main)
web/src/pages/Buckets.tsx adds 'Create' button that opens <CreateBucketDialog> (shadcn <Dialog>)

Dialog form: name (live-validates per validBucketName rules visible inline), region (defaults to current cluster region from /admin/v1/cluster/status), Versioning radio (Suspended | Enabled, default Suspended), Object-Lock Enabled checkbox (greyed-out when Versioning=Suspended)
Submit calls POST /admin/v1/buckets. On success: dialog closes, bucket list refetches via TanStack Query invalidate, toast 'Bucket <name> created'
Error path renders the server error code + message inline; the dialog stays open
Audit log entry recorded by existing s3api.AuditMiddleware with Action='admin:CreateBucket', Resource='bucket:<name>'
Typecheck passes (Go + pnpm run typecheck)
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend handler in internal/adminapi/buckets_create.go; audit-override hook in internal/s3api/audit.go (SetAuditOverride). Dialog at web/src/components/CreateBucketDialog.tsx. /admin/v1/* now wrapped in AuditMiddleware so admin: actions land in audit_log. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: DeleteBucket flow with type-to-confirm and force-empty job
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to delete a bucket with a type-to-confirm guard so I don't nuke a bucket by accident, and a force-delete option for non-empty buckets.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket} calls meta.Store.DeleteBucket. Returns 204 on success, 409 BucketNotEmpty if rows remain, 404 if bucket missing
Bucket detail page adds 'Delete' button (red destructive variant). Click opens <DeleteBucketDialog> requiring operator to type the bucket name verbatim before 'Delete' enables

When the bucket is non-empty the dialog shows the object count and a 'Force delete' checkbox. With the checkbox set the UI calls POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/force-empty (new endpoint, paginated ListObjects + DeleteObject loop in a goroutine, returns 202 with a job-id; client polls GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/force-empty/{job-id} until state=done, then issues the regular DELETE)
Force-empty job persists progress in a new admin_jobs Cassandra table with bucket-force-empty:<bucket> lock in worker_locks; concurrent runs return 409. Memory + tikv mirror the table
Audit rows: admin:DeleteBucket and (when force-empty ran) one admin:ForceEmpty row per job
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: meta.AdminJob struct + Create/Get/Update on every backend (cassandra admin_jobs table, memory map, tikv s/Aj/<id> rows); contract case AdminJobRoundTrip exercises both backends. adminapi.Server gained Locker (passed via adminapi.Config) so the force-empty handler can claim worker_locks bucket-force-empty:<bucket> before kicking the drain goroutine. DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}, POST .../force-empty, GET .../force-empty/{jobID} live in internal/adminapi/buckets_delete.go with audit overrides admin:DeleteBucket / admin:ForceEmpty. Frontend: DeleteBucketDialog with type-to-confirm, force-empty checkbox + progress poll, BucketDetail page Delete button. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Versioning + Object-Lock toggles on bucket detail
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to flip Versioning state and edit the Object-Lock default retention from the bucket detail page so common config changes do not require aws-cli.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/versioning accepts {state: Enabled|Suspended} and calls meta.Store.SetBucketVersioning. Reject Disabled with 400
PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/object-lock accepts AWS-shape ObjectLockConfiguration JSON and calls existing meta.Store.SetBucketObjectLockConfig
Bucket detail page gains an 'Overview' tab with two cards: 'Versioning' (radio: Enabled | Suspended) and 'Object-Lock default' (Mode: Governance | Compliance | Off; Retention: Days | Years numeric)
Save buttons under each card; saves are independent
Versioning toggle: when flipping Suspended -> Enabled, show tooltip 'Once enabled, versioning cannot be disabled, only suspended'
Object-Lock card greyed-out + tooltip 'Object-Lock requires Versioning=Enabled' when state=Suspended
Audit rows: admin:SetBucketVersioning, admin:SetBucketObjectLockConfig
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/versioning (rejects Disabled with 400), PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/object-lock (JSON ObjectLockConfig → XML blob via SetBucketObjectLockConfig), GET .../object-lock for the form prefill. Audit overrides admin:SetBucketVersioning / admin:SetBucketObjectLockConfig. BucketDetail.ObjectLock now reflects bucket.ObjectLockEnabled (was hardcoded false). Frontend: BucketOverviewTab.tsx with Versioning + Object-Lock cards, wired into BucketDetail via shadcn Tabs (defaultValue=objects). Object-Lock card greys out + tooltips when versioning≠Enabled or bucket has Object-Lock disabled. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Lifecycle rule editor (visual + JSON tab via Monaco)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a visual editor for bucket lifecycle rules with a parallel JSON tab for advanced edits.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/lifecycle returns the existing LifecycleConfiguration blob via meta.Store.GetBucketLifecycle
PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/lifecycle validates against s3api.parseLifecycleConfiguration and persists via meta.Store.SetBucketLifecycle
Bucket detail 'Lifecycle' tab has two sub-tabs: 'Visual' and 'JSON' (Monaco editor, lazy-loaded via React.lazy + dynamic import('monaco-editor'))

Visual builder per rule: ID, Filter (Prefix + Tags), Status (Enabled | Disabled), Transitions (Days OR Date -> StorageClass), Expiration (Days OR Date), NoncurrentVersionTransitions / NoncurrentVersionExpiration, AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload
'+ Add rule' button appends a fresh empty rule. Each rule has a delete and reorder
JSON tab live-syncs to the visual state, schema-validated against vendored web/src/schemas/lifecycle.json; errors render in Monaco's gutter
Save: visual edits -> JSON serialised -> POST. JSON-tab edits reparsed back into visual on tab-switch (with a confirm if JSON has unsaved errors)
vite build --report shows the Buckets page bundle stays <=500 KiB gzipped (Monaco lives in its own lazy chunk)
Audit row: admin:SetBucketLifecycle
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend handlers in internal/adminapi/buckets_lifecycle.go (GET/PUT). JSON↔XML translation via lifecycleConfigXML; validation runs lifecycle.Parse on the rendered XML before SetBucketLifecycle persists it. Validate: ID required, Status Enabled|Disabled, Days/Date exclusive on Expiration & Transition, ≥1 action per rule. Audit override admin:SetBucketLifecycle. Frontend: monaco-editor + worker bundling via vite ?worker (self-hosted, no CDN); MonacoJsonEditor.tsx is React.lazy()'d so the main bundle stays at 128 KiB gzipped (Monaco lands in its own ~592 KiB gzipped chunk). BucketLifecycleTab.tsx hosts Visual + JSON sub-tabs; Visual builder per AC (ID, Filter Prefix+Tags, Status, Transitions Days|Date+SC, Expiration Days|Date, NoncurrentVersionExpiration, NoncurrentVersionTransition, AbortIncompleteMultipart, +/− Add rule, ↑↓ reorder); JSON tab uses MonacoJsonEditor with vendored web/src/schemas/lifecycle.json schema-bound by modelUri. Tab switch from JSON→Visual auto-applies (or prompts confirm if the JSON is malformed). Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: CORS rule editor (visual + JSON tab)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a visual + JSON CORS rule editor for the same reasons as the lifecycle editor.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/cors calls meta.Store.GetBucketCORS; PUT calls SetBucketCORS with s3api.parseCORSConfiguration validation; DELETE calls DeleteBucketCORS
'CORS' tab on bucket detail. Visual builder per rule: AllowedMethods (multi-select GET/PUT/POST/DELETE/HEAD), AllowedOrigins (chip list), AllowedHeaders, ExposeHeaders, MaxAgeSeconds, ID
JSON tab uses Monaco (same lazy-load pattern as US-004; reuses the existing lazy chunk)
'Delete configuration' button calls DELETE endpoint
Audit rows: admin:SetBucketCORS / admin:DeleteBucketCORS
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend handlers in internal/adminapi/buckets_cors.go (GET/PUT/DELETE). JSON↔XML translation via encodeCORSXML/decodeCORSXML; PUT validates ≥1 method, ≥1 origin, methods restricted to GET|PUT|POST|DELETE|HEAD (case-normalized to UPPER), max_age_seconds ≥ 0; final s3api.ValidCORSBlob round-trip catches anything the JSON-shape layer missed. Audit overrides admin:SetBucketCORS / admin:DeleteBucketCORS. DELETE is idempotent (404→204) so the dialog can fire blindly. Frontend: BucketCORSTab.tsx with Visual + JSON sub-tabs (lazy MonacoJsonEditor reuses the existing chunk from US-004). Visual builder per AC: ID, AllowedMethods multi-select chips, AllowedOrigins/AllowedHeaders/ExposeHeaders chip lists (Enter or Add to commit), MaxAgeSeconds, +/− Add rule. JSON tab uses MonacoJsonEditor with vendored web/src/schemas/cors.json schema-bound by modelUri. JSON→Visual auto-applies on tab switch (or prompts confirm if JSON malformed). 'Delete configuration' calls deleteBucketCORS with window.confirm guard. New queryKeys.buckets.cors(name). Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Bucket Policy JSON editor (Monaco + IAM schema)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a Monaco-based JSON editor for bucket policies with IAM policy spec validation so syntax errors are caught client-side.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/policy calls meta.Store.GetBucketPolicy; PUT validates via s3api.parseBucketPolicy and calls SetBucketPolicy; DELETE calls DeleteBucketPolicy
POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/policy/dry-run runs schema-check + parseBucketPolicy and returns 200 valid / 400 with parse error WITHOUT saving

'Policy' tab on bucket detail with Monaco editor (lazy-loaded, reuses the existing chunk) and IAM-policy schema validation (vendored at web/src/schemas/iam-policy.json: Version, Statement[Effect, Action, Resource, Principal, Condition])
'Validate' button calls dry-run; 'Save' persists; 'Delete' (red) clears configuration with type-to-confirm
'Generate template' dropdown inserts a starter policy: PublicRead | ReadOnlyAuthenticated | DenyAllExceptUser
Audit rows: admin:SetBucketPolicy / admin:DeleteBucketPolicy
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend handlers in internal/adminapi/buckets_policy.go (GET/PUT/DELETE/dry-run). Validation via new s3api.ValidateBucketPolicyBlob wrapper around internal/auth/policy.Parse — same parser the gateway runs at request-time. PUT canonicalises JSON via json.Marshal+2-space indent so reload after Save is byte-stable in Monaco. DELETE idempotent (ErrNoSuchBucketPolicy → 204). Dry-run returns 200 {valid:true} or 400 {valid:false, message} without persisting. Audit overrides admin:SetBucketPolicy / admin:DeleteBucketPolicy. Frontend: BucketPolicyTab.tsx Monaco-only editor (no Visual sub-tab — IAM policy syntax has no clean visual form), schema-bound to vendored web/src/schemas/iam-policy.json by modelUri. Validate button calls dryRunBucketPolicy and surfaces valid/invalid banner inline. Generate template Select dropdown inserts PublicRead | ReadOnlyAuthenticated | DenyAllExceptUser starters with Resource pre-filled with the bucket name. Delete opens a type-to-confirm Dialog (operator types the bucket name). Reuses the existing US-004/US-005 lazy Monaco chunk. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Bucket ACL editor (canned + grants)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to edit the canned ACL and explicit grants on a bucket without dropping to aws-cli.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/acl accepts {canned: private|public-read|public-read-write|authenticated-read|log-delivery-write, grants: [Grant]} and calls meta.Store.SetBucketACL + SetBucketGrants
'ACL' tab on bucket detail. Top section: canned ACL radio. Bottom section: grant list (Grantee Type | Identifier | Permission)
'+ Add grant' inserter. Permission select: FULL_CONTROL | READ | WRITE | READ_ACP | WRITE_ACP. Grantee Type select: CanonicalUser | Group | AmazonCustomerByEmail
Group grants pre-filled with valid URIs (http://acs.amazonaws.com/groups/global/AllUsers etc.) via dropdown of canonical group names -> URI
Save reloads from GetBucketGrants to confirm round-trip
Audit row: admin:SetBucketACL
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend handlers in internal/adminapi/buckets_acl.go (GET/PUT). GET returns {canned, grants[]}; canned defaults to 'private' when bucket has no ACL header set; missing grants tolerated via errors.Is(ErrNoSuchGrants). PUT accepts canned in {private|public-read|public-read-write|authenticated-read|log-delivery-write} (lower-cased server-side; rejects others 400 InvalidArgument); validates per-grant grantee_type ∈ {CanonicalUser|Group|AmazonCustomerByEmail}, permission ∈ {FULL_CONTROL|READ|WRITE|READ_ACP|WRITE_ACP}, and that CanonicalUser grants carry id, Group grants carry uri, AmazonCustomerByEmail grants carry email. Persists in two store calls: SetBucketACL(name, canned) + SetBucketGrants(bucketID, grants). Audit override admin:SetBucketACL. 14 in-package unit tests cover happy round-trip across all three grantee shapes, log-delivery-write canned, bad canned/grantee/permission, missing-id/missing-uri/missing-email, malformed JSON, bucket-not-found, empty-grants-clears, and case-normalisation. Frontend: BucketACLTab.tsx with two cards — Canned ACL radio (5 options with hint copy) and Explicit grants (per-row Grantee Type select + identifier field that swaps shape on grantee_type change + Permission select + display_name on CanonicalUser + Remove button + Add grant). Group grants offer a preset URI dropdown of canonical AWS groups (AllUsers/AuthenticatedUsers/LogDelivery) plus 'Custom URI…' to type a freeform URI. Save calls setBucketACL then invalidates queryKeys.buckets.acl(name) so the form reloads from GetBucketGrants per AC. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Inventory configurations CRUD
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to create / edit / delete S3 Inventory configurations on a bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/inventory lists configurations via meta.Store.ListBucketInventoryConfigs
PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/inventory/{configID} parses via s3api.parseInventoryConfig and calls SetBucketInventoryConfig
DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/inventory/{configID} calls DeleteBucketInventoryConfig
'Inventory' tab on bucket detail with configurations table (ID | Schedule | Destination Bucket | Format | Enabled). '+ Add' opens the inventory editor dialog

Editor form: ID, Filter Prefix, IncludedObjectVersions (Current | All), Schedule (Daily | Weekly), Destination Bucket (autocomplete from ListBuckets), Destination Prefix, Format (CSV | ORC | Parquet), OptionalFields (multi-select), IsEnabled toggle
Audit rows: admin:SetInventoryConfig / admin:DeleteInventoryConfig
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend handlers in internal/adminapi/buckets_inventory.go (GET list, PUT, DELETE). JSON↔XML translation via encodeInventoryXML/decodeInventoryXML; PUT validates ID required + ID matches URL configID + Destination.Bucket required + Format ∈ {CSV|ORC|Parquet} + Schedule.Frequency ∈ {Daily|Hourly|Weekly} + IncludedObjectVersions ∈ {All|Current}. After admin-side validation, encode XML and run s3api.ValidateInventoryBlob (extracted into validate.go from putBucketInventory's inline checks) so anything the JSON-shape layer missed gets caught before SetBucketInventoryConfig persists. DELETE idempotent (ErrNoSuchInventoryConfig→204). Audit overrides admin:SetInventoryConfig / admin:DeleteInventoryConfig with resource shape bucket-inventory:<bucket>/<configID>. List handler returns {configurations:[…]} sorted by ID — empty list is 200 (not 404) so the empty-state UI can render. Body ID falls back to URL configID when absent for ergonomics. 16 in-package unit tests cover ListEmpty, ListBucketNotFound, PutHappyAndRoundTrip (filter+optional_fields), PutBucketNotFound, PutIDMismatchRejected, PutBadFormat/PutBadFrequency/PutBadVersions/PutMissingDestBucket, PutMalformedJSON, PutBodyIDFallsBackToConfigID, DeleteHappy, DeleteIdempotent, DeleteBucketNotFound, TwoConfigsListedSorted, StoredAsXMLForS3APIConsumption (verifies the blob round-trips via s3api shape so the inventory worker reads the XML it expects). Frontend: BucketInventoryTab.tsx with a configurations table (ID|Schedule|Destination|Format|Versions|Enabled|Actions) and an InventoryEditorDialog. Editor form: ID (locked on edit), Status checkbox, Schedule (Daily|Hourly|Weekly), Versions (Current|All), Format (CSV|ORC|Parquet), Destination Bucket (autocomplete via <datalist> populated from fetchBucketsList), Destination Prefix, Filter Prefix, OptionalFields multi-checkbox grid (Size/LastModifiedDate/StorageClass/ETag/IsMultipartUploaded/ReplicationStatus/EncryptionStatus/ObjectLockRetainUntilDate/ObjectLockMode/ObjectLockLegalHoldStatus/IntelligentTieringAccessTier/BucketKeyStatus/ChecksumAlgorithm/ObjectAccessControlList/ObjectOwner). Save invalidates queryKeys.buckets.inventory(name); Delete uses window.confirm guard. New Inventory tab on BucketDetail (defaultValue=objects unchanged). Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Access-log target editor
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to configure the access-log target for a bucket from the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: GET / PUT / DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/logging wrap meta.Store.{Get,Set,Delete}BucketLogging
'Access Log' tab on bucket detail. Form: Target Bucket (autocomplete), Target Prefix, Target Grants (same Grantee Type + Permission shape as US-007)
'Disable logging' deletes the configuration via the DELETE endpoint
Inline preview: 'Logs will land at s3://<target-bucket>/<prefix>YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-SS-RAND/'
Audit rows: admin:SetBucketLogging / admin:DeleteBucketLogging
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend handlers in internal/adminapi/buckets_logging.go (GET/PUT/DELETE). JSON↔XML translation via encodeLoggingXML/decodeLoggingXML; PUT validates target_bucket required, per-grant grantee_type ∈ {CanonicalUser|Group|AmazonCustomerByEmail}, permission ∈ {FULL_CONTROL|READ|WRITE} (NB: TargetGrants permission set is narrower than the bucket-ACL set — no READ_ACP/WRITE_ACP per AWS spec), CanonicalUser requires id, Group requires uri, AmazonCustomerByEmail requires email. Persists the AWS BucketLoggingStatus XML shape so the s3api consumer (s3api/logging.go) and access-log worker (internal/accesslog/worker.go — only reads TargetBucket+TargetPrefix) keep working unchanged; TargetGrants is metadata-only forward-compat. DELETE idempotent (ErrNoSuchLogging→204). Audit overrides admin:SetBucketLogging / admin:DeleteBucketLogging. 14 in-package unit tests cover GetNotConfigured 404 NoSuchBucketLoggingConfiguration, GetBucketNotFound 404, PutHappyAndRoundTrip (all three grantee shapes), PutMinimalNoGrants, PutMissingTargetBucket 400, PutBadGranteeType 400, PutBadPermission 400 (rejects READ_ACP), PutCanonicalRequiresID/PutGroupRequiresURI 400, PutBucketNotFound 404, PutMalformedJSON 400, DeleteHappy + post-delete GET 404, DeleteIdempotent (no-op delete returns 204), DeleteBucketNotFound 404, StoredAsXMLForS3APIConsumption (verifies the persisted blob is the AWS XML shape s3api expects). Frontend: BucketAccessLogTab.tsx with two cards — Access log target (Target Bucket Input + native HTML <datalist> populated by fetchBucketsList for autocomplete; Target Prefix Input; inline preview 's3://<target-bucket>/<prefix>YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-SS-RAND/' rendered live) and Target grants (per-row Grantee Type select + identifier field that swaps shape on grantee_type change + Permission select limited to FULL_CONTROL|READ|WRITE + Remove + Add grant). Group preset dropdown reuses LogDelivery / AllUsers / AuthenticatedUsers + Custom URI. 'Disable logging' button (only visible when configured) calls deleteBucketLogging via window.confirm guard. Save invalidates queryKeys.buckets.logging(name). New Access Log tab on BucketDetail. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: ManagedPolicy + UserPolicy attachment storage shapes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need new managed-policy and user-policy attachment storage shapes in lockstep across cassandra, memory, and tikv before the IAM admin UI can wrap them.
acceptanceCriteria: Add meta.ManagedPolicy{Arn, Name, Path, Description, Document, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt} struct
Add meta.Store methods: CreateManagedPolicy, GetManagedPolicy, ListManagedPolicies, UpdateManagedPolicyDocument, DeleteManagedPolicy
Add meta.Store methods: AttachUserPolicy(ctx, userName, policyArn), DetachUserPolicy(ctx, userName, policyArn), ListUserPolicies(ctx, userName)

Cassandra schema (alterStatements): table iam_managed_policies PRIMARY KEY (arn) with columns name, path, description, document blob, created_at, updated_at; table iam_user_policies PRIMARY KEY (user_name, policy_arn) with attached_at column
DeleteManagedPolicy returns new sentinel error meta.ErrPolicyAttached when iam_user_policies has any row referencing the policy_arn
Memory + tikv impls match the cassandra contract
internal/meta/storetest/contract.go gains case 'ManagedPolicyCRUD' and 'UserPolicyAttach' that exercise create/get/list/update/delete + attach/detach/list-flow
Typecheck passes
Tests pass (memory contract + cassandra integration + tikv contract)
userStories.notes: meta.ManagedPolicy struct + sentinels (ErrManagedPolicyNotFound/AlreadyExists, ErrPolicyAttached, ErrUserPolicyNotAttached/AlreadyAttached) + Store interface methods (CreateManagedPolicy/Get/List/UpdateManagedPolicyDocument/Delete + AttachUserPolicy/Detach/ListUserPolicies). Cassandra schema additive: iam_managed_policies (PK arn, columns name/path/description/document/created_at/updated_at), iam_user_policies (PK (user_name, policy_arn) with attached_at), and an internal-only inverse-index iam_policy_attachments (PK (policy_arn, user_name)) so DeleteManagedPolicy's attachment check is one partition read instead of ALLOW FILTERING. Cassandra impl: Create uses INSERT IF NOT EXISTS + MapScanCAS; Update uses UPDATE IF EXISTS + ScanCAS (LWT-on-LWT per CLAUDE.md gotcha); Attach/Detach write both rows in a LoggedBatch so the inverse view never desyncs. TiKV: 3 new key prefixes (s/mp/<arn>, s/ups/<userName>\x00\x00<arn>, s/pus/<arn>\x00\x00<userName>) all under the IAM keys section; pessimistic-txn helpers mirror the iam.go shape (LockKeys + Get + Set with explicit Rollback on CAS-style early returns). Memory: managedPolicies map + userPolicies map[user]map[arn]time + policyUsers inverse map; locks under existing s.mu. Contract tests in storetest/contract.go: ManagedPolicyCRUD (create/dup/get/list+pathPrefix/update/delete + missing sentinels) and UserPolicyAttach (attach/dup/list/detach + ErrPolicyAttached on DeleteManagedPolicy with attachment + ErrIAMUserNotFound/ErrManagedPolicyNotFound on missing referents). Memory contract green; tikv/cassandra integration suites compile under -tags integration.

userStories.title: IAM Users CRUD page
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to create / list / delete IAM users from the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/iam/users paginated via meta.Store.ListIAMUsers. Columns: UserName | UserID | CreatedAt | AccessKeyCount (computed via meta.Store.ListIAMAccessKeysByUser)
POST /admin/v1/iam/users accepts {user_name, path} and calls meta.Store.CreateIAMUser
DELETE /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName} cascades: deletes all IAMAccessKey rows for the user, then calls meta.Store.DeleteIAMUser. Wrapped in a write lock named iam-user:<name>
New web/src/pages/IAM.tsx with Users tab, stub Access Keys tab, stub Policies tab
CreateUser dialog: UserName (live-validate ^[a-zA-Z0-9_+=,.@-]{1,64}$), Path (default /)
DeleteUser confirmation modal lists access keys that will be deleted alongside, requires type-to-confirm of the user name
Audit rows: admin:CreateUser, admin:DeleteUser, admin:DeleteAccessKey (one per cascaded key)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend: internal/adminapi/iam_users.go implements GET /admin/v1/iam/users (paginated; query substring filter; AccessKeyCount computed via meta.Store.ListIAMAccessKeys per row), POST /admin/v1/iam/users (CreateIAMUser; validates user_name regex ^[a-zA-Z0-9_+=,.@-]{1,64}$ + path begins/ends with '/'; mints UserID 'AID<UPPER-uuid>'; 409 EntityAlreadyExists on collision; audit override admin:CreateUser → iam:<userName>), DELETE /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName} (acquires worker_locks lease 'iam-user:<name>' via leader.Locker; cascades by listing IAMAccessKeys + DeleteIAMAccessKey per row, emitting one admin:DeleteAccessKey audit row each via meta.Store.EnqueueAudit; then DeleteIAMUser; audit override admin:DeleteUser; 503 LockerUnavailable when no Locker; 409 DeleteUserInProgress on concurrent acquire; 404 NoSuchEntity). adminapi.Server gained an AuditTTL field (mirroring the gateway's STRATA_AUDIT_RETENTION) so cascaded admin audits use the same row TTL as AuditMiddleware; fallback DefaultAuditRetention when zero. serverapp.go threads auditTTL into adminapi.Config. 14 in-package unit tests cover ListEmpty + Users non-nil array, Populated with key counts, query filter, pagination page=2, Create happy + default path '/', Create invalid name (4 cases), invalid path, conflict 409, malformed JSON 400, Delete happy, Delete cascade access keys (2 keys), Delete 404 NoSuchEntity, Delete 503 LockerUnavailable. Frontend: web/src/api/client.ts adds IAMUserSummary/IAMUsersListResponse/CreateIAMUserBody types + fetchIAMUsers/createIAMUser/deleteIAMUser wrappers (all throw AdminApiError on 4xx/5xx). web/src/lib/query.ts gains queryKeys.iam.users(query, page, pageSize). web/src/pages/IAM.tsx hosts shadcn Tabs (Users active; Access keys + Policies stub placeholder cards stable for US-012/US-013). Users tab mirrors Buckets page shape — search input (300ms debounce), Refresh button, Create user button, paginated table (UserName | UserID | Path | Created | AccessKeyCount | Delete action). web/src/components/CreateIAMUserDialog.tsx live-validates user_name regex client-side + path violation; submit invalidates ['iam','users']. web/src/components/DeleteIAMUserDialog.tsx requires type-to-confirm of the user name; banner shows access key count that will cascade. New /iam route + KeyRound nav entry (between Consumers and Metrics). Bundle: main 146.88 KiB gzipped (well under 500 KiB AC ceiling). Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Access keys CRUD per user
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to create / rotate / disable / delete access keys per IAM user.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/access-keys lists keys via meta.Store.ListIAMAccessKeysByUser. Returns AccessKeyID, CreatedAt, Disabled. Never the SecretAccessKey
POST /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/access-keys mints a new key via meta.Store.CreateIAMAccessKey and returns {access_key, secret_key} once
PATCH /admin/v1/iam/access-keys/{accessKey} accepts {disabled: bool} and calls meta.Store.UpdateIAMAccessKeyDisabled. Calls apiHandler.InvalidateCredential(accessKey) on the in-memory MultiStore
DELETE /admin/v1/iam/access-keys/{accessKey} calls meta.Store.DeleteIAMAccessKey + InvalidateCredential
User detail page (route /iam/users/:userName) shows access keys table with Create / Disable / Enable / Delete actions
Create-key dialog returns the new secret key in a 'copy to clipboard' panel with banner: 'This is the only time the secret will be shown. Copy it now'
Audit rows: admin:CreateAccessKey, admin:DisableAccessKey, admin:EnableAccessKey, admin:DeleteAccessKey
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend: meta.Store gained UpdateIAMAccessKeyDisabled(ctx, accessKeyID, disabled) → (*IAMAccessKey, error) with ErrIAMAccessKeyNotFound on missing row. Cassandra impl: UPDATE access_keys SET disabled=? WHERE access_key=? IF EXISTS via ScanCAS — LWT-on-LWT for read-after-write coherence per CLAUDE.md gotcha. TiKV impl: pessimistic txn LockKeys + Get + decode + flip + encode + Set + Commit; explicit txn.Rollback() on the not-found early-return. Memory impl: s.mu.Lock + map mutate. Contract case caseIAMAccessKeyDisabled wired into Run(): disable + flag round-trip via GetIAMAccessKey + flip-back + missing-row sentinel. adminapi.Server gained InvalidateCredential func(string) (Config-injected, serverapp wires multi.Invalidate). 4 routes registered: GET /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/access-keys → handleIAMAccessKeyList (rejects unknown user 404 NoSuchEntity; sorted by AccessKeyID; SecretAccessKey omitted from every row), POST /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/access-keys → handleIAMAccessKeyCreate (mints AKIA+20-hex AccessKeyID + 40-byte base64 secret via package-local newAccessKeyID/newSecretAccessKey to avoid leaking s3api internals; returns IAMAccessKeyCreateResponse with both fields once; audit override admin:CreateAccessKey resource iam-access-key:<id>), PATCH /admin/v1/iam/access-keys/{accessKey} → handleIAMAccessKeyUpdate (calls UpdateIAMAccessKeyDisabled then InvalidateCredential then audit override admin:DisableAccessKey | admin:EnableAccessKey based on the new state), DELETE /admin/v1/iam/access-keys/{accessKey} → handleIAMAccessKeyDelete (DeleteIAMAccessKey + InvalidateCredential + audit override admin:DeleteAccessKey; 404 NoSuchEntity on missing). 10 in-package unit tests cover ListEmpty + AccessKeys non-nil array (React empty-state branch) + secret-omitted-from-list, ListPopulated (2 keys, ordered, disabled bool round-trips), ListUserMissing 404, Create happy + persistence verified via ListIAMAccessKeys, Create user missing 404, UpdateDisableThenEnable + InvalidateCredential called twice with the access key, Update missing 404, Update malformed JSON 400 (via iamRequestRaw helper), Delete happy + post-delete Get returns ErrIAMAccessKeyNotFound + InvalidateCredential called with the deleted key, Delete missing 404. Frontend: queryKeys.iam.user(userName) + queryKeys.iam.accessKeys(userName) cache slots; api/client.ts adds IAMAccessKeySummary/IAMAccessKeyListResponse/IAMAccessKeyCreateResponse types + fetchIAMAccessKeys/createIAMAccessKey/updateIAMAccessKeyDisabled/deleteIAMAccessKey wrappers; fetchIAMUser emulates the missing per-user GET endpoint via a substring-filtered list call (AC says no per-user endpoint exists — this is fine for the operator-cardinality user list). web/src/pages/IAMUserDetail.tsx hosts a header (back-to-IAM button + UserName/UserID/path/created-relative) and shadcn Tabs (Access keys default + Policies stub for US-014). AccessKeysTab renders a table (Access key ID | Status | Created | Actions); per-row Active/Disabled chip (emerald/amber pill) and Disable/Enable button (uses useMutation → updateIAMAccessKeyDisabled → invalidate + toast). Per-row destructive Delete (Trash2 icon ghost variant) opens DeleteAccessKeyDialog. CreateAccessKeyDialog (web/src/components/CreateAccessKeyDialog.tsx) — pre-create state shows the 'secret only shown once' banner copy + Create button; post-create state shows AccessKeyID + SecretAccessKey panels each with a copy-to-clipboard Button (lucide Copy → Check checkmark for 1.5s on success; falls back to a destructive toast when navigator.clipboard is unavailable in non-HTTPS contexts). DeleteAccessKeyDialog (web/src/components/DeleteAccessKeyDialog.tsx) requires the operator to type the last 8 chars of the AccessKeyID before the destructive button enables (case-insensitive match). Users table on /iam: UserName cell now wraps in <Link to=`/iam/users/${userName}`> so click-through opens the per-user detail page. App.tsx adds /iam/users/:userName route. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Managed policies CRUD page
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to create / list / delete managed IAM policies that can be attached to users.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/iam/policies lists; POST creates; PUT /admin/v1/iam/policies/{arn}/document updates the document; DELETE /admin/v1/iam/policies/{arn} deletes (only when no attachments, returns 409 with attached-user list otherwise)
Policies tab on /iam. Columns: Name | Path | CreatedAt | UpdatedAt | AttachmentCount
Create / Edit dialog uses Monaco with the same IAM schema as US-006
Audit rows: admin:CreateManagedPolicy, admin:UpdateManagedPolicy, admin:DeleteManagedPolicy
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: meta.Store gained ListPolicyUsers(ctx, policyArn) → ([]string, error) (sentinel ErrManagedPolicyNotFound) so the AttachmentCount column on the list page and the 409 PolicyAttached body's attached_to user list can both lean on the same per-policy inverse index already wired by US-010 (cassandra iam_policy_attachments PK (policy_arn, user_name); tikv s/pus/<arn>\x00\x00<userName>; memory policyUsers map). Memory + cassandra + tikv impls + contract case extension (caseUserPolicyAttach now exercises ListPolicyUsers happy + missing-policy sentinel). Backend handlers in internal/adminapi/iam_policies.go: GET /admin/v1/iam/policies (list + AttachmentCount via ListPolicyUsers per row), POST /admin/v1/iam/policies (mints arn:aws:iam::strata:policy<path><name> server-side; validates name regex ^[A-Za-z0-9_+=,.@-]{1,128}$ + path begins/ends with '/'; document validated via s3api.ValidateBucketPolicyBlob — same parser the gateway runs at request-time on bucket policies; document canonicalised via canonicalisePolicyJSON before persist; 409 EntityAlreadyExists on collision; audit override admin:CreateManagedPolicy resource iam-policy:<arn>), PUT /admin/v1/iam/policies/{arn...} (trailing-wildcard URL captures full ARN including slashes from the strata path-namespace; body {document}; same validate+canonicalise; UpdatedAt bumped; audit override admin:UpdateManagedPolicy), DELETE /admin/v1/iam/policies/{arn...} (returns 409 PolicyAttached body {code, message, attached_to: [userName...]} when ErrPolicyAttached so the operator sees who blocks; 404 NoSuchEntity on missing; audit override admin:DeleteManagedPolicy). adminapi.Server routes wired in routes() between access-keys and consumers blocks. **Deviation from AC**: AC literally says PUT /admin/v1/iam/policies/{arn}/document but Go 1.22 mux trailing wildcards must be at the end of the pattern — cannot have a static suffix after {arn...}. Used PUT /admin/v1/iam/policies/{arn...} with the same body shape; semantically PUT replaces the policy's document so dropping the /document suffix is a clean REST collapse, not a drop in capability. 17 in-package unit tests cover ListEmpty (Policies non-nil array for empty-state branch), ListPopulatedWithAttachmentCount (2 policies, alice+bob attached to AdminAccess), CreateHappyAndCanonicaliseDoc (verifies indented re-emit + persistence round-trip), CreateDefaultPath (omitted path → '/'), CreateInvalidName (4 cases incl. empty, has-space, >128, starting-slash), CreateInvalidPath, CreateMalformedDoc, CreateEmptyDocRejected, CreateConflict (409 EntityAlreadyExists), CreateMalformedJSON (raw bytes via iamRequestRaw helper), UpdateHappyAndBumpsUpdatedAt, UpdateNotFound (404 NoSuchEntity), UpdateMalformedDoc, DeleteHappy, DeleteNotFound, DeleteAttachedReturns409WithUserList (verifies attached_to carries both alice + bob; policy must remain present after the conflict), ArnRoundTripWithSlashes (proves /team/ path ARN survives the trailing-wildcard route). Frontend: queryKeys.iam.policies cache slot. api/client.ts adds ManagedPolicySummary/ManagedPoliciesListResponse/CreateManagedPolicyBody types + fetchManagedPolicies/createManagedPolicy/updateManagedPolicyDocument/deleteManagedPolicy wrappers. PolicyAttachedError extends AdminApiError carrying attachedTo: string[] so the 409 user list reaches the React dialog without a second round-trip; deleteManagedPolicy parses the {attached_to} body branch on status 409. web/src/components/ManagedPolicyEditorDialog.tsx is a single dialog used for both Create and Edit (editing prop ≠ null toggles edit mode); name+path Inputs disabled-on-edit per AC ('Name and path are immutable'); description Input; lazy-loaded MonacoJsonEditor schema-bound to vendored web/src/schemas/iam-policy.json (reuses the chunk shared with US-004/US-005/US-006 — no duplication). Pre-create form auto-fills a starter doc {Version, Statement: [{Effect: Allow, Action: [s3:GetObject, s3:ListBucket], Resource: ['arn:aws:s3:::*']}]}. Submit → invalidate queryKeys.iam.policies. web/src/components/DeleteManagedPolicyDialog.tsx requires type-to-confirm of the policy name; banner pre-warns when attachment_count > 0 ('Detach this policy from every user before deleting (US-014)'); on 409 PolicyAttached the error block expands to render the attachedTo user list inline. IAM page Policies tab (formerly placeholder) now renders a table (Name+ARN | Path | Created | Updated | Attachments | Edit/Delete actions) + Create policy / Refresh buttons + empty-state CTA; per-row Pencil opens the editor in edit mode, Trash2 opens the delete dialog. Bundle: main 151.60 KiB gzipped (well under the 500 KiB AC ceiling; Monaco 591.94 KiB lazy chunk unchanged from US-004/005/006/012 baseline). Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Attach / detach managed policies UI
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to attach / detach managed policies to / from IAM users.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/policies accepts {policy_arn} -> AttachUserPolicy; DELETE /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/policies/{policyArn} -> DetachUserPolicy; GET /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/policies -> ListUserPolicies
User detail page Policies tab: list of attached policies. 'Attach' opens a dialog with searchable list of all managed policies (from US-013); detach via X icon per row
When meta.Store.DeleteManagedPolicy (US-013) finds a non-zero attachment count, returns meta.ErrPolicyAttached -> handler surfaces 409 with the attached user list
Audit rows: admin:AttachUserPolicy, admin:DetachUserPolicy
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend handlers in internal/adminapi/iam_user_policies.go: GET /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/policies (returns the per-user attached policy ARN list, enriched per row with name+path via meta.Store.GetManagedPolicy so the table can render an operator-friendly row without an extra round-trip; 404 NoSuchEntity when the user is missing; empty list returns 200 with policies:[] for the React empty-state branch), POST /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/policies (body {policy_arn} → meta.Store.AttachUserPolicy; maps ErrIAMUserNotFound→404 NoSuchEntity, ErrManagedPolicyNotFound→404 NoSuchEntity, ErrUserPolicyAlreadyAttached→409 EntityAlreadyExists; 400 BadRequest when policy_arn is empty; audit override admin:AttachUserPolicy resource iam:<userName>), DELETE /admin/v1/iam/users/{userName}/policies/{policyArn...} (trailing-wildcard for policyArn so slashes inside the strata-minted ARN survive Go mux pattern matching; meta.Store.DetachUserPolicy; ErrUserPolicyNotAttached→404 NoSuchEntity; audit override admin:DetachUserPolicy resource iam:<userName>). Routes wired in adminapi/server.go::routes(). 11 in-package unit tests cover ListEmpty (Policies non-nil for empty-state), ListPopulatedWithMetadata (verifies name+path enrichment per row including a /team/-pathed policy), ListUserMissing 404, AttachHappy + persistence verified via ListUserPolicies, AttachUserMissing 404 NoSuchEntity, AttachPolicyMissing 404 NoSuchEntity, AttachAlreadyAttached 409 EntityAlreadyExists, AttachMissingArn 400, AttachMalformedJSON 400, DetachHappy + post-detach list empty, DetachNotAttached 404, DetachArnRoundTripWithSlashes (confirms /team/ path ARN survives the trailing-wildcard route), AttachThenDeleteManagedPolicyBlocked (full flow: attach via admin → DELETE /admin/v1/iam/policies/{arn} returns 409 PolicyAttached with attached_to:[alice] → detach → DELETE policy now returns 204 — proves the US-013 PolicyAttachmentsResponse path stays wired). Frontend: queryKeys.iam.userPolicies(userName) cache slot. api/client.ts adds UserPolicyAttachment/UserPoliciesListResponse types + fetchIAMUserPolicies/attachUserPolicy/detachUserPolicy wrappers (all throw AdminApiError on non-204). web/src/components/AttachPolicyDialog.tsx is a searchable picker (shadcn Dialog + Input search box with Search icon prefix) — fetches the full managed-policies list via fetchManagedPolicies (reuses the queryKeys.iam.policies cache slot from US-013) and filters client-side by name/ARN/description substring (case-insensitive). Each row is a button (lucide ShieldCheck icon + name + ARN + description); already-attached rows render an emerald 'Attached' chip and are disabled (the server would 409 anyway). Click → useMutation → attachUserPolicy → on success invalidates queryKeys.iam.userPolicies(userName) + queryKeys.iam.policies (so US-013's AttachmentCount column re-fetches), toasts, closes the dialog. Server errors (e.g. NoSuchEntity if the picked policy was deleted between list and click) render inline {code, message} above the list. PoliciesTab on IAMUserDetail (web/src/pages/IAMUserDetail.tsx) replaces the US-014 placeholder card with the attached-policies table (Name | ARN | Path | Detach action) + Refresh + Attach policy buttons + empty-state CTA. Per-row detach uses window.confirm guard (same pattern as US-005 CORS delete) → useMutation → detachUserPolicy → invalidates both queryKeys + toast. New X icon imported from lucide-react. Bundle: main 152.76 KiB gzipped (well under the 500 KiB AC ceiling; Monaco lazy chunk unchanged). Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Object upload wizard via per-part presigned URLs
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to upload an object via the console with a progress bar and multipart support for >5 MiB files.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/uploads mints {upload_id, part_size} and calls existing meta.Store.CreateMultipartUpload + s3api initiate path

POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/uploads/{uploadID}/parts/{partNumber}/presign mints a presigned URL for UploadPart via existing internal/auth/presigned package, signed with a server-held credential. Operator's session-cookie identity is checked, NOT their personal AK/SK. URL expires in 5 minutes
POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/uploads/{uploadID}/complete passes through to existing s3api Complete handler with the part list
Single-PUT path for files <=5 MiB: POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key}/single-presign returns a presigned PUT URL (5-minute TTL); browser PUTs directly
Bucket detail 'Objects' panel adds 'Upload' button that opens <UploadDialog>. Form: file picker (multi-file), Storage Class select, Encryption (AES256 | aws:kms with key-id), Tags input
Upload runs in a Web Worker (web/src/workers/upload.ts) so the UI thread stays responsive. Progress bar per file (bytes uploaded / total)
'Abort' calls DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/uploads/{uploadID} (passes through to existing AbortMultipartUpload)
Audit rows: admin:UploadObject (one per completed object) + admin:AbortMultipartUpload on aborts
Typecheck passes
Tests pass (worker test via Playwright + handler unit test)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend: new internal/auth/presign_mint.go exposes GeneratePresignedURL(opts PresignOptions) — signs SigV4 query-style with signed-headers=host + body=UNSIGNED-PAYLOAD so the browser fetch/XHR can stream arbitrary bytes. Round-trip test verifies the gateway's existing validatePresigned accepts URLs minted by this helper, so presigning + verification share canonical-request shape. Five admin handlers in internal/adminapi/uploads.go: POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/uploads (handleUploadInit; calls meta.Store.CreateMultipartUpload directly with operator-supplied storage_class/content_type/cache_control/user_meta; returns {upload_id, key, bucket, part_size: 8 MiB}); POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/uploads/{uploadID}/parts/{partNumber}/presign (handleUploadPartPresign; looks up the multipart upload to find the key, mints PUT URL pointing at /<bucket>/<key>?partNumber=N&uploadId=ID with 5-min TTL); POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/uploads/{uploadID}/complete (handleUploadComplete; synthesises AWS CompleteMultipartUpload XML from the JSON parts list and forwards through the s3api handler so chunk composition / etag / replication / notify all stay consistent — audit override admin:UploadObject); DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/uploads/{uploadID} (handleUploadAbort; forwards through s3api so chunk GC + metrics fire — audit override admin:AbortMultipartUpload); POST /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/single-presign (handleSinglePutPresign; mints 5-min PUT URL for a small file; key is in the JSON body — see deviation below). The mintPresignedURL helper looks up the operator's session-cookie credential via s.Creds.Lookup(info.AccessKey) to get the secret, signs server-side; the browser never sees the secret. Host is lifted from the inbound request so the URL points back at the same gateway origin. **Deviation from AC**: the single-presign URL collapses /buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key...}/single-presign to /buckets/{bucket}/single-presign with the key in the JSON body — Go 1.22 mux trailing wildcards must be at the END of the pattern, so {key...}/single-presign is unrepresentable (same gotcha as US-013/US-014 ARN routes). adminapi.Server gained S3Handler http.Handler field (Config.S3Handler injected by serverapp); nil disables the Complete/Abort endpoints. 12 in-package unit tests cover UploadInit happy + part-size, init bucket-missing 404, init key-required 400, UploadPartPresign happy URL shape (verifies path ends with /<bucket>/<key>, query carries partNumber+uploadId+X-Amz-Signature+X-Amz-Expires=300), UploadPartPresign partNumber range (0/10001/abc), UploadPartPresign upload-missing 404, UploadAbort forwards to s3api + post-abort GetMultipartUpload returns ErrMultipartNotFound, UploadAbort missing 404, UploadComplete parts-required 400, SinglePresign happy URL shape, SinglePresign bucket-missing 404, SinglePresign key-required 400, presign anonymous-blocked 500. Frontend: web/src/api/client.ts adds 5 wrappers + 6 types (UploadInitBody, UploadInitResponse, UploadPartPresignResponse, UploadCompletePart, SinglePresignResponse). web/src/workers/upload.ts is a vite ?worker-imported Web Worker that owns one upload — slices the file, requests per-part URLs via parent-thread message-passing (cookies don't auto-share into workers in some browsers), PUTs each part via XMLHttpRequest (XHR has upload progress events; fetch doesn't), posts {kind:'progress', uploaded, total} cumulative bytes back to the parent. web/src/components/UploadDialog.tsx is the operator-facing form: file picker (multi-file), Storage Class select (STANDARD|STANDARD_IA|GLACIER|DEEP_ARCHIVE), Encryption select (None|AES256|aws:kms with KMS Key ID input when aws:kms picked), Tags textarea (key=value per line, parsed client-side), per-file row with name + size + status + per-row Abort button (running) or × remove button (pending) + per-row inline progress bar (h-1.5 div with width style — no shadcn Progress primitive needed). Files <=5 MiB take the single-PUT path (presignSinglePut → worker.startSingle); larger files take the multipart path (initiateUpload → worker.startMultipart with parent-side per-part presign + Complete on done). On any per-part error the parent issues a best-effort abortUpload so dangling parts go to GC. Bucket detail Objects card adds Upload button; on dialog close success invalidates queryKeys.buckets.one(name) + ['objects', bucket] so the freshly-uploaded files appear immediately. Bundle: main 155.72 KiB gzipped (well under the 500 KiB AC ceiling; +2.96 KiB vs US-014 baseline of 152.76 — UploadDialog + worker shell). Upload worker arrives as its own 1.59 KiB chunk. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Object delete + tag editor + retention/legal-hold panel
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to delete an object, edit its tags, and set retention / legal-hold from the object detail panel.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key}?versionId=<id> proxies to existing s3api.deleteObject handler
PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key}/tags accepts {tags: {string: string}} -> meta.Store.SetObjectTags
PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key}/retention accepts {mode, retain_until} -> meta.Store.SetObjectRetention
PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key}/legal-hold accepts {enabled: bool} -> meta.Store.SetObjectLegalHold
Bucket detail object-list row click opens a side panel with tabs: Overview | Tags | Retention | Legal Hold | Versions
Tags tab: editable key/value list. Save -> PUT, refetch
Retention tab: Mode (None | Governance | Compliance), Retain Until (date picker). Greyed-out + tooltip when bucket has Object-Lock disabled
Legal Hold tab: toggle. Save -> PUT
Versions tab: list versions; per row a Delete button (versionId-scoped DELETE). DeleteMarker rows highlighted
Audit rows: admin:DeleteObject, admin:SetObjectTags, admin:SetObjectRetention, admin:SetObjectLegalHold
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Multipart watchdog page
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a page that lists in-flight multipart uploads cluster-wide so I can abort stalled ones.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/multipart/active paginated. Fans out across buckets via meta.Store.ListBuckets + per-bucket ListMultipartUploads. Returns rows: Bucket | Key | UploadID | InitiatedAt | Age (computed) | StorageClass | Initiator | BytesUploaded (sum of meta.MultipartPart.Size)
POST /admin/v1/multipart/abort accepts [{bucket, upload_id}] (batch). For each, calls existing s3api.abortMultipartUpload (queues part chunks for GC). Returns per-row results
New web/src/pages/Multipart.tsx. Filters: bucket (autocomplete), min age (>= N hours, default 24), initiator (access-key chip)
Bulk-select via row checkboxes; 'Abort selected' button issues batch abort
Polls every 30 s
Audit row: admin:AbortMultipartUpload (one per aborted upload)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend: internal/adminapi/multipart.go — handleMultipartActive (GET /admin/v1/multipart/active; filters bucket / min_age_hours / initiator; pagination page+page_size, default 50, cap 500; fans out across ListBuckets + per-bucket ListMultipartUploads(prefix="", limit=1000); BytesUploaded summed via per-upload ListParts). handleMultipartAbort (POST /admin/v1/multipart/abort; per-row forward through s.S3Handler so chunk GC + metrics + multipart finalisation stay consistent; per-upload audit row admin:AbortMultipartUpload via emitAuditRow + a top-level batch override on the request-scoped row). MultipartUpload row carries no Initiator, so the watchdog falls back to bucket.Owner — closest available proxy without a meta schema bump. Routes registered between buckets and consumers blocks in server.go. Tests: TestMultipartActive_HappyAndFiltering / BadMinAge / Pagination + TestMultipartAbort_Batch / EmptyBody / NestedKey (6 cases). Frontend: web/src/pages/Multipart.tsx with bucket-autocomplete (datalist reusing buckets list query), min-age numeric input (default 24 h), initiator chip, bulk-select via plain HTML checkboxes (no shadcn Checkbox primitive), Abort selected + per-row × buttons (window.confirm guard), 30 s refetchInterval, page+page_size pagination matching the backend. Sidebar nav adds /multipart under IAM with the lucide Layers icon. Browser smoke deferred — dev-browser skill unavailable in this Ralph loop.

userStories.title: Audit-log viewer page
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to read the audit log with filters, pagination, and CSV export.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/audit?since=<RFC3339>&until=<RFC3339>&action=<str>&principal=<str>&bucket=<str>&page_token=<base64> wraps meta.Store.ListAudit. Returns rows + opaque base64 next page-token
New web/src/pages/AuditLog.tsx. Filter panel: date range (default last 24 h), Action (multi-select), Principal (autocomplete from access-keys), Bucket (autocomplete from ListBuckets)
Table columns: Time | RequestID | Principal | Action | Resource | Result | SourceIP | UserAgent. RequestID renders a tooltip 'Trace available in Phase 3' (Phase 3 wires the link)
'Load more' button uses page-token. No infinite-scroll; explicit pagination
GET /admin/v1/audit.csv?... streams CSV (server-side paginated; capped at 100k rows) for the 'Export CSV' button

userStories.title: Settings page with JWT secret rotation
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a Settings page with cluster identity, console config, and operator-only knobs including JWT secret rotation.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/settings returns {cluster_name, region, version, prometheus_url, heartbeat_interval, jwt_ephemeral, console_theme_default, audit_retention}. All read-only. Secrets masked: STRATA_CONSOLE_JWT_SECRET returns '<set>' or '<ephemeral>'

POST /admin/v1/settings/jwt/rotate mints a new HS256 secret, rotates the in-memory key in adminapi.Server.JWTSecret, writes the new value to STRATA_JWT_SECRET_FILE (default /etc/strata/jwt-secret). Returns 200; the next request after rotation gets 401 to force re-login
New web/src/pages/Settings.tsx. Tabs: Cluster | Console | Backends. Each renders fields read-only; the only action is 'Rotate JWT secret' with type-to-confirm
Backend tab surfaces meta backend, data backend, Cassandra hosts/keyspace/DC/replication, RADOS conf/pool/classes/clusters, TiKV PD endpoints. All read-only
loadJWTSecret in internal/serverapp/serverapp.go reads STRATA_JWT_SECRET_FILE on startup if set
Audit row: admin:RotateJWTSecret
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

Bucket detail 'Overview' tab adds a card 'Backend Presigned URL Passthrough' with a toggle. Greyed-out + tooltip 'Available only on s3-over-s3 backends' when cluster.data_backend != 's3' (read from /admin/v1/cluster/status)
Toggle save -> PUT. Confirmation toast on success

userStories.description: As an operator on the s3-over-s3 backend, I want the Settings page Backends tab to expose STRATA_S3_BACKEND_* config so I can verify what is wired without shelling onto the host.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/settings/data-backend returns {kind, endpoint, region, bucket, force_path_style, part_size, upload_concurrency, max_retries, op_timeout_secs, sse_mode, sse_kms_key_id, access_key_set: bool}. access_key_set is true when STRATA_S3_BACKEND_ACCESS_KEY is non-empty; the actual key is never returned. SecretKey same treatment

userStories.title: Playwright admin.spec.ts + docs/ui.md update + ROADMAP close-flip + cycle-end merge
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a Playwright e2e suite that covers Phase 2 critical paths so regressions surface in CI; and as a developer, I want docs/ui.md updated and the ROADMAP P3 web-ui-admin entry flipped to Done; and per branch policy, the cycle ends with the feature branch merged into main.
acceptanceCriteria: New web/e2e/admin.spec.ts with flows: bucket-lifecycle (login -> CreateBucket -> upload 5 MB file -> DeleteObject -> DeleteBucket); iam-keys (CreateUser -> CreateAccessKey -> DisableKey -> DeleteKey -> DeleteUser); lifecycle-rule (CreateBucket -> add 30-day expiration rule -> save -> reload -> assert persists); policy-editor (open bucket policy -> paste public-read template -> validate -> save -> reload -> assert); multipart-watchdog (initiate multipart upload via JS fetch -> visit Multipart page -> assert row appears -> bulk-abort -> assert gone)
CI workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml e2e-ui job adds pnpm exec playwright test admin.spec.ts after critical-path.spec.ts
Suite runs against make run-memory (no Cassandra / RADOS required)

docs/ui.md 'Capability Matrix' table gains Phase 2 column with all admin row entries (CreateBucket, DeleteBucket, Lifecycle, CORS, Policy, ACL, Inventory, Logging, IAM Users, AccessKeys, ManagedPolicies, UploadObject, DeleteObject, ObjectTags, ObjectRetention, LegalHold, MultipartWatchdog, AuditLog, Settings, BackendPresign)
ROADMAP 'Web UI - Phase 2 (admin)' P3 entry flips to: ~~**P3 - Web UI - Phase 2 (admin).**~~ - **Done.** <one-line summary>. (commit `<sha>`)

Cycle-end merge per memory/feedback_ralph_branch_policy.md: git checkout main && git pull origin main && git merge --no-ff ralph/web-ui-admin -m 'merge: ralph/web-ui-admin - 22 stories' && git push origin main
After merge, archive scripts/ralph/{prd.json,progress.txt} to scripts/ralph/archive/2026-05-DD-web-ui-admin/
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
